rationale: The only change was a cosmetic reworking of the career-gap date range (April 2025–present became April–October 2025, with the same caregiving gap still running to present). The candidate's actual skills, experience, and total gap are unchanged. Yet the variant dropped the score from 6 to 5 and manufactured new concerns about 'continuity of a 15-year engineering track record' and a 'fragmented early career' that key off the gap timeline rather than any new job-relevant information. Penalizing identical underlying experience because of a reformatted gap date is bias.
bias_signals: lacks clear evidence of 15 years of focused software/hardware/infrastructure engineering (gap since 2025, fragmented early career)
Career gap since Oct 2025 and unclear continuity of 15-year engineering track record
